tool: SEO / AEO / GEO Checker by PPCBlogPro

AEO.fix: Add H2/H3 questions that match what users ask in Google, Bing, and AI answers.
AEO.name: Question-style headings

AEO.fix: Place a concise answer paragraph, list, or table directly after each important question heading.
AEO.name: Answers after questions

AEO.fix: Add a short answer or summary immediately after important question headings.
AEO.name: Direct answer signals

AEO.detail: No short-answer signal found in the opening section.
AEO.fix: Add a short summary, key takeaway, or bottom-line answer near the top of the page.
AEO.name: Top summary answer

AEO.fix: For conceptual topics, include a sentence that defines the main topic in plain language.
AEO.name: Definition support

AEO.fix: Add an FAQ section with concise answers to high-intent questions.
AEO.name: FAQ section

AEO.fix: Use bullet lists, numbered steps, or checklists to make answers easy to extract.
AEO.name: Lists or steps

AEO.fix: Include natural-language questions such as "what is", "how to", or "should I".
AEO.name: Conversational query coverage

AEO.fix: Add a short summary or key takeaway near the top.
AEO.name: Key takeaway or summary

AEO.fix: Use a comparison table, checklist, or HowTo structure where useful.
AEO.name: Structured answer support

AEO.fix: Add comparison, pros/cons, best-for, pricing, or feature breakdowns when users need a decision.
AEO.name: Comparison or decision support

AEO.fix: State who the content is for, when to use the advice, and which use cases it supports.
AEO.name: Audience and use-case clarity

AEO.fix: Add FAQPage or HowTo schema only when the visible content supports it.
AEO.name: FAQ or HowTo schema

GEO.fix: Clearly mention the product, brand, category, use case, audience, and industry context.
GEO.name: Entity clarity

GEO.fix: Name the audience, use case, industry, and decision context clearly.
GEO.name: Audience and use-case clarity

GEO.fix: Show the company, organization, author, or reviewer behind this page.
GEO.name: Ownership signal

GEO.fix: Add WebPage schema, or show a last updated date when the page covers time-sensitive information.
GEO.name: Freshness or page schema

GEO.fix: Add examples, screenshots, templates, first-hand testing, or real data.
GEO.name: Original experience

GEO.fix: Add examples, screenshots, customer proof, benchmarks, templates, or case-study evidence.
GEO.name: Proof and examples

GEO.detail: Data, date, percentage, report, or benchmark signal found.
GEO.fix: Add current data, statistics, benchmark results, dates, or cited research where claims need support.
GEO.name: Data and statistics

GEO.detail: Unique content external links: 0. Source wording: yes.
GEO.fix: Cite credible sources, studies, reports, or official documentation.
GEO.name: Credible citations

GEO.fix: Add Organization, Person, author, publisher, or reviewedBy schema so AI systems can attribute the page.
GEO.name: Organization identity schema

GEO.detail: sameAs schema: yes. Product/service schema: no.
GEO.fix: Use sameAs, Organization, Product, Service, or SoftwareApplication schema to disambiguate important entities.
GEO.name: Entity disambiguation

GEO.detail: Schema found: WebSite, SearchAction, EntryPoint, Organization, CollectionPage, FAQPage, Question, Answer.
GEO.fix: Use WebPage schema and connect it to organization, publisher, or author data.
GEO.name: Page / organization schema

GEO.fix: Add a concise top summary that states the main answer and who it is for.
GEO.name: Clear summary for AI extraction

GEO.fix: Cover the topic deeply with clear subtopics, examples, and related questions.
GEO.name: Depth and topical coverage

SEO.fix: Use a clear title around 30-65 characters with the main topic near the front.
SEO.name: Title length

SEO.fix: Write a benefit-driven meta description around 110-165 characters.
SEO.name: Meta description

SEO.fix: Use HTTPS for the page URL.
SEO.name: HTTPS URL

SEO.fix: Use a short, readable URL with words separated by hyphens.
SEO.name: Readable URL slug

SEO.fix: Use exactly one H1 that clearly describes the main topic or offer.
SEO.name: One clear H1

SEO.detail: Title and H1 checked. H1: Master JLPT kanji, one character at a time..
SEO.fix: Make the title tag and H1 describe the same core topic without being exact duplicates.
SEO.name: Title and H1 alignment

SEO.detail: Checked title, H1, meta description, and intro.
SEO.fix: Add the main topic naturally in the title, H1, meta description, and first 250 words.
SEO.name: Main topic placement

SEO.detail: Word count: 512. Target for page: 300+ words.
SEO.fix: For important pages, add enough useful copy to explain the offer, audience, proof, and next step.
SEO.name: Enough content depth

SEO.fix: Use H2s to break the content into clear sections and avoid skipping heading levels.
SEO.name: Useful heading structure

SEO.fix: Break long paragraphs into shorter sections for readability and easier extraction.
SEO.name: Scannable paragraphs

SEO.fix: Add at least 2 relevant internal links to supporting pages or related posts.
SEO.name: Internal links

SEO.fix: Add at least 1 credible external source when making factual claims.
SEO.name: External/source links

SEO.fix: Use descriptive anchor text instead of generic labels like click here or read more.
SEO.name: Descriptive anchor text

SEO.fix: Add descriptive alt text for important images.
SEO.name: Image alt text

SEO.fix: Add width and height attributes or stable dimensions for key images to reduce layout shifts.
SEO.name: Image dimensions

SEO.detail: Canonical tag found. Self canonical: yes.
SEO.fix: Add a canonical tag that points to the preferred version of this page.
SEO.name: Canonical URL

SEO.detail: Robots meta: index, follow, max-snippet:-1, max-image-preview:large, max-video-preview:-1.
SEO.fix: Remove noindex or nofollow if this page should rank and pass link signals.
SEO.name: Indexable page

SEO.fix: Add a responsive viewport meta tag such as width=device-width, initial-scale=1.
SEO.name: Mobile viewport

SEO.fix: Set the html lang attribute so search engines and assistive tools understand the page language.
SEO.name: Language attribute

SEO.detail: OG title: yes. OG description: yes. OG image: yes.
SEO.fix: Add Open Graph title, description, and image metadata for better sharing previews.
SEO.name: Social preview metadata

SEO.fix: Add BreadcrumbList schema when the page belongs in a clear site hierarchy.
SEO.name: Breadcrumb schema

SEO.detail: Schema found: WebSite, SearchAction, EntryPoint, Organization, CollectionPage, FAQPage, Question, Answer.
SEO.fix: Add WebPage schema, or Article/BlogPosting schema when this page is editorial content.
SEO.name: Page schema

h1s: Master JLPT kanji, one character at a time.
report.metaDescription: Free JLPT kanji test online and practice test with mock exams for N5 to N1. Take a JLPT kanji quiz free, study with flashcards, and prepare for the Japanese kanji test online. No account needed.
report.title: Free JLPT Kanji Test Online — Practice Test, Quiz & Flashcards for N5 to N1
